query_used: Symptoms: hypotension (moderate; BP 92/58), altered mental status (moderate; confused and drowsy), oliguria (severe; urine output 15 mL/hr). Abnormal labs: Lactate 3.8 mmol/L elevated, Creatinine 1.9 mg/dL elevated, WBC 18.7 K/uL elevated. Retrieve the most relevant ICU clinical guideline evidence, thresholds, and diagnostic criteria for the flagged risks.

citations.rationale: SEPSIS_RISK matched findings: hypotension, altered mental status, oliguria, lactate > 2 mmol/L, WBC > 12 K/uL, creatinine elevated. Retrieved guideline from Sepsis-3 Consensus Definitions supports this risk. Relevant evidence: SECTION 1 Sepsis-3 Definitions and Diagnostic Criteria Source: Singer M, et al. The Third International Consensus Definitions for Sepsis and Septic Shock (Sepsis-3). JAMA. 2016;315
citations.threshold: Sepsis-3: suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase >= 2

citations.rationale: SEPTIC_SHOCK_RISK matched findings: persistent hypotension, lactate > 2 mmol/L. Retrieved guideline from Septic Shock Identification and Management supports this risk. Relevant evidence: Septic shock requires sepsis plus persistent hypotension needing vasopressors to maintain MAP >=65 mmHg and lactate >2 mmol/L despite adequate fluid resuscitation.
citations.threshold: Septic shock: vasopressor-dependent hypotension with MAP >= 65 and lactate > 2 mmol/L after fluids

citations.rationale: AKI_RISK matched findings: oliguria, creatinine elevated. Retrieved guideline from KDIGO AKI Guidelines 2012 supports this risk. Relevant evidence: KDIGO AKI definition: creatinine rise >=0.3 mg/dL in 48 hours, or >=1.5x baseline in 7 days, or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/hour for >=6 hours.
citations.threshold: KDIGO AKI: creatinine rise >= 0.3 mg/dL in 48h or urine output < 0.5 mL/kg/hr for >= 6h

citations.rationale: EARLY_WARNING_ESCALATION matched findings: hypotension, altered mental status. Retrieved guideline from NEWS2 Early Warning Score supports this risk. Relevant evidence: SECTION 9 National Early Warning Score 2 (NEWS2) Source: Royal College of Physicians. National Early Warning Score (NEWS) 2. Updated report of a working party. London: RCP, 2017. 9
citations.threshold: NEWS2 high risk: score >= 7 or severe parameter abnormalities requiring urgent review
